data.title: Cementos Pacasmayo projects sustained EBITDA margins at 27% in 2025 amid volume growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cementos Pacasmayo (NYSE:CPAC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Humberto Nadal announced the retirement of CFO Manuel Ferreyros effective March 31, 2025, with Eli Hayashi stepping in as the new CFO. This transition is part of a succession plan initiated over three years ago.

  • Sales volumes increased 2.6% year-over-year for Q4 2024, driven by public sector sales and contributions from the Piura airport project. Full-year revenues rose 1.4%, supported by a dynamic pricing strategy despite a slight decrease in sales volumes.

  • Consolidated EBITDA for 2024 reached PEN549.3 million, with a record EBITDA margin of 27.8% for the year. Nadal emphasized confidence in achieving even stronger results in 2025, given a favorable demand environment.

  • Nadal highlighted the company’s progress in digital transformation, including AI-driven solutions, logistics optimization, and ISO 27001 certification for cybersecurity. These initiatives aim to enhance efficiency and innovation.

  • CFO Manuel Ferreyros reported Q4 revenues of PEN526.7 million, a 3% increase year-over-year. However, gross profit declined 2.1% due to higher costs tied to the Piura airport project.

Outlook

  • Nadal stated that sales volumes are expected to continue on a positive trend in 2025, reflecting growth seen in Q4 2024. He highlighted strong employment in agriculture and fishing sectors as key drivers for residential construction demand.

  • Sustaining capital expenditures are projected at PEN100 million for both 2025 and 2026, with no additional CapEx anticipated.

  • Management aims to maintain EBITDA margins similar to the 27% level achieved in Q4 2024, highlighting stable energy costs and effective pricing strategies.

Financial Results

  • EBITDA for Q4 2024 was PEN142.5 million, with a margin of 27.1%. Full-year EBITDA increased 14% compared to 2023, driven by operational efficiencies and cost optimization.

  • Administrative expenses rose 16% year-over-year for the quarter, while selling expenses increased 34.8%, attributed to higher personnel costs and third-party service fees.

  • Cement sales increased 1.8% in Q4 due to higher average prices. For the full year, cement sales volumes decreased 2.2%, impacted by reduced demand in the self-construction segment, but gross profit and margin increased due to cost controls.

  • Concrete and mortar sales grew 30.9% in Q4 and 48.8% for the year, primarily due to demand from the Piura airport project. However, gross margins faced pressure due to cost overruns and currency exchange rate discrepancies in the project.

  • Net debt-to-EBITDA ratio improved to 2.7x, reflecting continued deleveraging efforts.

Q&A

  • Marcelo Furlan, Itau: Asked about sales volumes for 2025, higher Q4 costs, and capital allocation. Nadal responded that sales volumes should show positive trends, sustaining CapEx will remain at PEN100 million, and higher costs were tied to personnel and profit-sharing expenses but are manageable within the current EBITDA margin.

  • Adrian Huerta, JP Morgan: Inquired about energy costs and segment-specific volume trends. Ferreyros noted stable energy costs for 2024 and 2025, with local coal for kilns and long-term electricity contracts. Nadal emphasized that employment levels and infrastructure spending are key drivers of demand.

  • Francisco Suarez, Scotiabank: Questioned cement utilization rates and EBITDA margin sustainability. Nadal highlighted 70% clinker utilization and stable energy and personnel costs as factors supporting margin consistency.

  • Luis Ramos, Larrain Vial: Asked about vulnerabilities in projected volume recovery and Piura airport cost overruns. Nadal stated that cost overruns are fully accounted for and highlighted employment growth as a positive demand signal.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about volume growth and EBITDA margins, focusing on cost pressures and project execution challenges. Questions indicated moderate skepticism regarding margin sustainability.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing operational efficiencies and strategic initiatives. Nadal’s repeated assurances on stable margins and volume growth reflected strong confidence.

  • Compared to Q3 2024, management’s tone remained consistent, with continued emphasis on digital transformation and operational efficiencies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sales volumes moved to a positive trajectory in Q4 2024, reversing declines in Q3. Full-year revenues also improved due to pricing strategies.

  • EBITDA margins declined from the 29.9% achieved in Q3 to 27.1% in Q4, reflecting higher costs tied to the Piura project.

  • Analysts in Q4 raised more detailed questions about cost pressures and volume recovery compared to Q3, reflecting a stronger focus on near-term risks.

  • Management’s strategic focus on digital transformation and building solutions remained unchanged quarter-over-quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Cost overruns on the Piura airport project impacted gross margins, but management confirmed no further negative effects in 2025.

  • Vulnerability to security issues and election-related uncertainties was noted but deemed manageable by management.

  • Analysts raised concerns about sustaining EBITDA margins and the financial impact of large infrastructure projects.

Final Takeaway

Cementos Pacasmayo delivered a strong Q4 2024 performance, marked by record EBITDA margins and revenue growth, despite cost pressures from the Piura airport project. Management showcased confidence in sustaining margins and achieving volume growth in 2025, supported by stable energy costs, strong employment in key sectors, and infrastructure investments. The transition to a new CFO was emphasized as part of a well-structured succession plan. Overall, strategic initiatives in digital transformation and building solutions position the company for continued growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: JFrog, Procore surge after quarterly results, analysts react
data.description:
Shares of Procore Technologies (NYSE:PCOR) and JFrog (NASDAQ:FROG) jumped following their quarterly results, while Cohu (COHU) dipped about 1% on Friday.

Procore Technologies (NYSE:PCOR): Procore's stock surged about 14% after the company reported fourth quarter results and outlook.

Canaccord kept its Buy rating and raised the price target to $97 from $83 on the shares of Procore, which provides a cloud-based construction management platform.

Piper Sandler maintained its Overweight rating on the stock and increased the price target to $110 from $90. Meanwhile, Barclays reiterated its Equal Weight rating and raised the price target to $90 from $84.

In addition, Procore saw its price target increased to $95 from $82 at TD Cowen, and at Scotiabank to $95 from $85. KeyBanc also increased the price target on the shares to $96 from $85.

JFrog (NASDAQ:FROG): Shares of JFrog, which provides a hybrid software supply chain platform, rose about 5% on Friday after fourth quarter results beat estimates. Barclays maintained its Overweight rating and raised the price target on the stock to $48 from $38.

Needham kept its Buy rating but increased the price target to $46 from $39. Morgan Stanley reiterated its Overweight rating on the shares and upped the price target to $44 from $36.

Canaccord maintained its Buy rating, while raising the price target to $45 from $38. The company saw its price target increased to $47 from $37 at TD Cowen, and to $40 from $30 at Scotiabank.

KeyBanc raised the price target to $46 from $36, while Cantor increased it to $46 from $38. Stifel upped price target on JFrog's stock to $45 from $35.

Cohu (COHU): Cohu, which provides semiconductor test equipment and services, saw its stock fall about 1% on Friday after fourth quarter results missed estimates.

TD Cowen reduced the price target on the shares to $27 from $30, while Stifel cut the price target to $35 from $40.

Meanwhile, Needham maintained its Hold rating on Cohu's stock.

data.title: Galapagos downgraded at Morgan Stanley on new pipeline focus
data.description:
Shares of Galapagos (NASDAQ:GLPG) (OTCPK:GLPGF) ended a four-day winning streak on Friday after Morgan Stanley downgraded the Belgian biotech, citing its decision to deprioritize its CAR-T therapy GLPG5201 in favor of a similar asset called GLPG5101.

Frommer trimmed Galapagos’ (GLPS) probability of success against chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) and Richter’s Transformation (RT) following the decision announced with the company’s Q4 2024 results on Wednesday.

Noting that CLL and RT represented the key value drivers in his model for GLPG, Frommer argued that GLPG5101 has yet to generate clinical data against both indications where rivalry for a new CD-19 targeting CAR-T approach appears low.

With potential catalysts more than a year away, the analyst downgraded Galapagos (NASDAQ:GLPG) to Underweight from Equal Weight and slashed its price target to $22 from $31 per share, hoping for additional clinical activity within the company’s GLPG5101 program.

data.title: PG&E cut to Sell equivalent at Morgan Stanley on high fire risk, liability exposure
data.description:
PG&E (NYSE:PCG) -1.7% in Friday's trading as Morgan Stanley downgrades shares to Underweight from Equal Weight with a $16.50 price target, trimmed from $20, joining California utility peer Edison International (NYSE:EIX) with the bank's Sell-equivalent rating, in the wake of the recent Eaton fire.

California utilities are again exposed to severe financial risk, and significant valuation discounts will persist until there is clarity on the state's wildfire fund, alongside lingering tail risk of another catastrophic fire, Morgan Stanley analyst David Arcaro says.

The analyst estimates the damages from the Eaton fire could result in payouts of as much as $13.5B from the $21B wildfire fund, meaning that the fund couls no longer support another catastrophic fire at Eaton's level of damages, leaving California utility shareholders exposed to open-ended liabilities from the next Eaton-sized fire, with no cap available if a utility is judged to have been responsible.

A large fire can happen anytime, as evidenced by the off-season mid-January timing of the wind-related events that started the Eaton and Palisades fires, and the chance of an extreme weather event or catastrophic fire damage is impossible to quantify with any real precision, yet it is critical to the financial outlook for the stocks, Arcaro notes.

The analyst says he would wait for progress on a replenishment mechanism of the wildfire fund before getting more comfortable with the tail risk of California utilities.

data.title: Enbridge targets $8B-$9B annual growth projects amid strong 2025 guidance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Enbridge Inc. (NYSE:ENB) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Greg Ebel highlighted record EBITDA and DCF per share for 2024, driven by new assets and strong customer demand, contributing to a 13% EBITDA increase over 2023. The company also increased its dividend for the 30th consecutive year and achieved a 37% total shareholder return in 2024.

  • Enbridge closed the acquisition of three premier U.S. natural gas utilities, creating the largest gas utility franchise in North America. Additionally, three Permian and Gulf Coast asset acquisitions were finalized, expanding its oil and gas footprint.

  • Over $8 billion in organic projects were added to the backlog, diversified across its business units, with a current total capital backlog of $26 billion. Major projects include a $1.2 billion Aspen Point T-North expansion and a 2.5 Bcf/day Blackcomb Pipeline in the Permian Basin.

  • CFO Pat Murray reported Q4 EBITDA of over $5.1 billion and adjusted EPS of $0.75, marking a 17% increase year-over-year. Murray reaffirmed 2025 guidance, expecting adjusted EBITDA between $19.4 billion and $20 billion and DCF per share between $5.50 and $5.90.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed 2025 guidance of adjusted EBITDA between $19.4 billion and $20 billion and DCF per share between $5.50 and $5.90, supported by full-year contributions from recent acquisitions and new assets in service.

  • Enbridge plans $8 billion to $9 billion in annual growth project capacity, prioritizing organic projects with high returns and quick cycles, with a focus on gas distribution, transmission, and renewables.

  • CEO Greg Ebel emphasized confidence in continuing growth through the decade, supported by trends such as data center demand, re-industrialization, and LNG export growth.

Financial Results

  • Q4 adjusted EPS was $0.75 compared to analysts' estimate of $0.74. Revenues were not explicitly disclosed in the transcript.

  • Liquids EBITDA benefited from toll escalators and strong throughput on Gulf Coast and Mid-Continent assets. Gas Transmission saw significant contributions from the Whistler JV and new projects like the Venice Extension Project.

  • Gas Distribution saw an approximate $500 million year-over-year EBITDA increase, reflecting the first full quarter of contributions from the acquired U.S. utilities.

Q&A

  • Jeremy Tonet, JPMorgan: Asked about Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin expansion opportunities. CEO Greg Ebel stated ongoing customer discussions for expansions targeting 2026-2027, emphasizing low capital and high-return projects.

  • Robert Catellier, CIBC: Inquired about Enbridge’s appetite for long-haul pipelines in Canada. Ebel noted significant legislative and regulatory changes would be required to pursue such projects.

  • Ben Pham, BMO Capital Markets: Asked about solar return rates. Matthew Akman, President of Renewable Power, highlighted mid-teens returns due to strong customer partnerships and quick-cycle capital deployment.

  • Theresa Chen, Barclays: Queried about the potential impacts of tariffs on operations. Management indicated minimal volume impacts but noted costs might be shared depending on region and refining options.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing heavily on growth opportunities and potential regulatory challenges. Questions were pointed but constructive, reflecting interest in the company’s long-term strategy.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks and Q&A, consistently reaffirming guidance and emphasizing strategic positioning. Statements like "we are confident" and "strong returns" underscored their optimism.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, discussions reflected increasing confidence in growth opportunities, particularly in renewables and gas distribution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance reaffirmation remained consistent, though language around data center and power demand growth was more assertive this quarter.

  • Analysts’ focus shifted toward data center opportunities and regulatory risks, compared to prior discussions more centered on acquisitions.

  • Management tone exhibited increased confidence in capturing opportunities from data center growth and quick-cycle projects.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential regulatory delays, especially around LNG-related projects like Rio Bravo, were highlighted. Management expressed confidence in navigating these challenges.

  • Analysts raised concerns about tariff impacts and their implications for cross-border trade and economic growth.

  • Management emphasized mitigation strategies, including focusing on permit-light, high-return projects and leveraging their diversified portfolio.

Final Takeaway

Enbridge Inc. delivered record financial performance for Q4 2024, supported by strong asset utilization, strategic acquisitions, and consistent dividend growth. Management reaffirmed its 2025 guidance and outlined a robust $8 billion to $9 billion annual growth project capacity, focusing on quick-cycle, high-return investments across gas transmission, distribution, and renewables. Analysts remained attentive to regulatory risks and tariff implications, while management emphasized confidence in long-term growth opportunities through data center demand, LNG expansion, and re-industrialization trends.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ingersoll Rand targets 3%-5% revenue growth in 2025 with $300M M&A impact
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ingersoll Rand (NYSE:IR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Vicente Reynal highlighted strong 2024 performance, including double-digit adjusted EPS growth and robust free cash flow margins, supported by the company's economic growth engine. He emphasized ongoing investments in organic and inorganic growth, with over 200 active M&A targets.
  • Reynal stated the company expanded its total addressable market by $12 billion in 2024 through acquisitions and expects inorganic revenue growth of 400 to 500 basis points in 2025.
  • CFO Vik Kini reported Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.84, and full-year adjusted EPS of $3.29, reflecting an 11% year-over-year increase. Adjusted EBITDA reached near-record margins of 28% in Q4.

Outlook

  • Revenue is projected to grow by 3%-5% in 2025, with organic growth anticipated at 1%-3%. Pricing is expected to contribute 75% of this growth, with the remainder from volume.
  • Adjusted EBITDA is forecasted between $2.13 billion and $2.19 billion, while adjusted EPS is expected to range from $3.38 to $3.50. Corporate costs are estimated at $165 million for the year.
  • M&A is projected to contribute $300 million in revenue for 2025, reflecting completed and closed transactions, including the acquisitions of SSI Aeration and Excelsior Blower Systems.

Financial Results

  • Q4 orders were up 8%, and revenue increased by 4%, achieving a book-to-bill ratio of 0.95. For the full year, orders rose 4%, and revenue grew 5%, with a book-to-bill ratio of 0.98.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $532 million, representing a 6% year-over-year improvement. Full-year adjusted EBITDA margin reached a record 27.9%, up 190 basis points.
  • The PST segment experienced a 14% year-over-year increase in adjusted EBITDA to $107 million in Q4, despite challenges in Aerospace & Defense and China.

Q&A

  • Michael Halloran, Baird: Asked about demand assumptions in the 2025 guidance. Reynal explained that organic growth is expected to be flat in the first half, with 4% growth in the second half driven by pricing and volume improvements.
  • Julian Mitchell, Barclays: Inquired about Q4 orders and China’s impact. Reynal attributed softness to delayed large orders but noted stability in China’s book-to-bill ratio at approximately 1.
  • Nigel Coe, Wolfe Research: Queried about PST margins and 2025 outlook. Kini anticipates PST margins to improve sequentially in 2025, reaching 30% by year-end.
  • Chris Snyder, Morgan Stanley: Asked about delayed projects due to site readiness. Reynal identified permitting and logistical challenges but noted no cancellations.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern over project delays, particularly in China and Aerospace & Defense, though they acknowledged stability in other regions.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing the strength of its economic growth engine and robust M&A pipeline. Reynal stated, "We see stability" when discussing China, and Kini highlighted "strong operational execution."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone appeared slightly more cautious due to elongated decision-making cycles and site readiness issues.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for organic revenue growth in 2025 is consistent with the cautious optimism expressed in Q3, with continued focus on pricing as a growth driver.
  • M&A activity remains a priority, with the number of active targets increasing from 10 in Q3 to over 200 in Q4.
  • Management sentiment shifted slightly as project delays and long decision cycles were emphasized more prominently than in Q3.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from general macroeconomic challenges in Q3 to specific concerns about execution risks in delayed projects and regional performance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Project delays due to site readiness and engineering constraints were highlighted as key challenges.
  • China’s softness and its impact on order conversions remain a concern, though management noted stability in book-to-bill ratios.
  • Aerospace & Defense margins were pressured by volume declines, though restructuring and integration efforts are expected to improve performance in 2025.
  • Analysts questioned the potential for tariff-related costs, with Kini outlining mitigation strategies, including regional sourcing and pricing adjustments.

Final Takeaway

Ingersoll Rand delivered solid financial results in Q4 2024, driven by strong operational execution and strategic acquisitions. While project delays and China’s softness present short-term challenges, the company remains well-positioned for 2025 with a projected revenue growth of 3%-5% and continued focus on M&A to enhance its market presence. Management’s confidence in its economic growth engine and strategic initiatives underscores its commitment to delivering long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Aker outlines 5% dividend growth for Aker BP in 2025 amid strong cash flow and portfolio streamlining
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Aker ASA (OTCPK:AKAAF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Øyvind Eriksen, President and CEO, highlighted the current market dynamics, emphasizing global volatility and the shift toward protectionism. He noted that Aker's net asset value reached NOK58.2 billion at year-end, reflecting portfolio strength despite uncertainties. The company successfully executed transactions worth NOK27 billion during 2024, distributing NOK32 billion in dividends across portfolio companies.

  • Eriksen announced significant developments, including the liquidation plans for Philly Shipyard and AMSC following strategic sales, and Solstad Maritime’s IPO slated for Q2 2025. Aker Horizons also finalized the sale of 80% of its carbon capture business, enabling a NOK3.5 billion dividend distribution.

  • Aker BP was highlighted as a cornerstone of the portfolio, maintaining strong operational performance with a low-cost portfolio of $6.2 per barrel and CO2 intensity of 2.6 kilograms per barrel. The company is set to maintain over 500,000 barrels per day of production into 2030, bolstered by increased Johan Sverdrup recovery and Yggdrasil field development. Eriksen emphasized Aker BP’s pioneering digitalization and AI-driven operational advancements, including the world’s first unmanned production platform.

  • CFO Svein Oskar Stoknes reported that fair value-adjusted assets reached NOK65.4 billion at quarter-end, with the net asset value at NOK56.2 billion, equaling NOK756 per share. The company’s net interest-bearing debt declined to NOK2 billion, supported by a robust liquidity buffer of NOK8.4 billion. Stoknes called attention to upstream cash inflows of NOK5.1 billion and debt repayment totaling NOK1.9 billion during the quarter.

Outlook

  • Aker announced a proposed dividend of NOK26.5 per share for 2024, with a potential second tranche under review for later in 2025. This follows the NOK51 per share distributed in 2024. The company reaffirmed its dividend policy of 4% to 6% of net asset value, but left room for flexibility based on financial strength and investment capacity.

  • Eriksen noted that Aker BP plans a 5% base dividend growth for 2025, supported by production growth and robust operational cash flow.

Financial Results

  • Aker’s total liabilities stood at NOK9.2 billion at year-end, primarily composed of bond debt and bank loans. The company’s equity book value rose to NOK25 billion, driven by NOK4.9 billion in profit before tax and offset by dividends paid.

  • Dividend income in Q4 totaled NOK5.1 billion, including NOK4.1 billion from Aker Solutions and NOK888 million from Aker BP. The company’s cash holdings ended at NOK617 million, with significant outflows for dividends, debt repayment, and equity investments.

  • Cognite, a key software portfolio company, recorded a 40% increase in annual recurring revenue, reaching $94 million by year-end, driven by strong commercial momentum and expansion into new industry segments.

Q&A

  • Christina Schartum: "Øyvind, you mentioned Aker Horizons and the ongoing process to optimize the company's capital structure. Can you say a bit more about this process and Aker's view on repayment of debt?" Øyvind Eriksen: "Aker Horizons is focused on monetizing assets, distributing proceeds, and refinancing debt during 2025."

  • Christina Schartum: "What is the latest on Cognite’s IPO preparation?" Øyvind Eriksen: "Cognite’s growth and relocation to the U.S. align with its IPO readiness strategy, though no specific timeline has been set."

  • Christina Schartum: "What do you see as the main value triggers in Aker going forward?" Øyvind Eriksen: "Aker BP remains a key driver with long-term production growth and dividend potential. Additionally, Cognite and ACE offer significant software portfolio opportunities."

  • Christina Schartum: "Aker’s dividend policy indicates a level above the 4% to 6% range. Will this policy be updated?" Øyvind Eriksen: "The policy remains unchanged, but the Board retains flexibility to propose higher dividends based on financial strength and opportunities."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about capital optimization, IPO readiness, and dividend sustainability, reflecting cautious optimism. Questions focused on tangible returns and growth potential, signaling slightly positive sentiment.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing resilience, strategic flexibility, and shareholder value creation. Eriksen reiterated clear plans for growth and innovation, using phrases like "well positioned" and "significant value creation."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone from both analysts and management showed greater focus on execution and operational advancements, reflecting continued progress.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted slightly, with Q4 emphasizing dividend flexibility and portfolio monetization, while Q3 centered on enhancing dividend policies and streamlining investments.

  • Analysts in Q4 focused heavily on IPOs, dividend flows, and capital optimization, compared to Q3’s focus on oil price impacts and Aker BP’s operational performance.

  • Management’s tone remained consistent, but Q4 reflected greater emphasis on digital innovation (Cognite, Aker BP) and portfolio realignments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Eriksen acknowledged geopolitical uncertainties and protectionist market dynamics as key risks, with Aker's strategy focusing on resilience and diversification.

  • Challenges in Aker Solutions' renewable ventures, such as inflation and supply chain disruptions, prompted a shift toward selective contract models and simplification.

  • Analysts expressed concerns over capital allocation in Aker Horizons and delays in Aker Carbon Capture’s dividend distribution, which management addressed with detailed explanations.

Final Takeaway

Aker ASA continues to prioritize shareholder value through strategic divestments, dividend growth, and operational efficiency. With production increases from Aker BP, Cognite’s strong revenue momentum, and a balanced approach to portfolio optimization, the company is positioned to navigate geopolitical uncertainties and deliver consistent returns to investors.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Short sellers boost bets against energy stocks in January
data.description:
Short sellers increased their bearish bets against S&P 500 oil and gas stocks in January compared to a month ago.

In January 2025, the crude oil futures ticked up by 0.8%, compared to a near 5% rise in the same period last year. Prices have taken a hit from expectations of a global surplus amid economic slowdown and tariff threats on crude. President Trump said U.S. tariffs would apply to crude, although he may reduce levies on Canadian oil from 25% to 10% beginning around February 18.

A bunch of S&P500 energy companies also reported their quarterly results, with the majority posting an earnings beat.

The average short interest in the S&P 500 energy sector stocks increased to 2.44% at the end of January from 2.35% at December end. The short bets, in which traders sell borrowed shares of companies with the aim of buying them back later at a lower price, rose to 484.35M from 467.85M in December.

The S&P 500 Energy sector (NYSEARCA:XLE) ticked up 1.1% in January, compared to the near 3% rise in the benchmark index S&P500 (SP500). In the year 2024, the energy sector ETF lagged the broader market, up only 1.1% compared to the S&P benchmark index, which gained 24% in 2024.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions (Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float)

Most shorted S&P 500 energy stocks as of Jan-end

  • APA Corporation (APA) 7.33% vs 6.15% in the prior month.
  • Texas Pacific Land (TPL) 6.97% vs 6.14%.
  • Schlumberger (SLB) 5.13% vs 3.55%.
  • Valero Energy (VLO) 3.94% vs 4.84%.
  • ONEOK (OKE) 3.93% vs 2.67%.

Least shorted S&P 500 energy stocks as of Jan-end

  • Exxon Mobil (XOM) 0.92% vs 0.92% in the prior month.
  • ConocoPhillips (COP) 1.03% vs 0.93%.
  • Baker Hughes Company (BKR) 1.38% vs 1.38%.
  • The Williams Companies (WMB) 1.43% vs 1.47%.
  • Phillips 66 (PSX) 1.61% vs 1.55%.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Oil & Gas Equipment and Services was the most shorted industry within the sector last month, with 3.46% short interest as of January end, replacing Oil & Gas E&P.

On the contrary, Oil & Gas Integrated was the least shorted industry within the energy sector, with 1.64% short interest as of January end.

data.title: Dundee Precious Metals targets $200M share repurchases and advances Čoka Rakita to feasibility study completion by 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dundee Precious Metals (OTCPK:DPMLF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • David Rae, President and CEO, highlighted the company's strong performance in 2024, achieving gold production and cost guidance for the 10th consecutive year. He emphasized advancements in the Čoka Rakita project, with the prefeasibility study completed in 2024 and a feasibility study targeted for year-end 2025. He noted the project’s potential to contribute 170,000 ounces of gold annually in its first five years of production.
  • Rae detailed exploration milestones, including two discoveries near Čoka Rakita and plans for $23M to $25M in exploration spending in 2025. Additionally, the Chelopech mine life extension remains a priority, with increased exploration budgets focused on near-mine targets.
  • Navin Dyal, CFO, reported record free cash flow of $305M for 2024, contributing to a cash position exceeding $800M by early 2025. "We are targeting up to $200M in share repurchases during 2025," said Dyal, affirming the company's commitment to capital returns.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its three-year guidance, projecting average annual production of 200,000 ounces of gold and 30 million pounds of copper from 2025 to 2027. All-in sustaining costs are expected to average $865 per ounce of gold sold during this period.
  • The Čoka Rakita feasibility study is set to conclude by year-end 2025, with a construction start targeted for mid-2026 and production planned for 2028. The company also allocated $12M to $14M for Loma Larga updates, with a feasibility study expected in Q2 2025.
  • Gold production at Ada Tepe is projected to decline by about 50% in the first half of 2025 due to mine sequencing but will recover in the second half.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for 2024 reached $607M, driven by favorable metal prices and lower treatment charges at Chelopech. Adjusted net earnings were $232M or $1.29 per share. Free cash flow totaled $305M for the year.
  • All-in sustaining costs for the year averaged $872 per ounce of gold sold, reflecting lower gold volumes and higher labor costs, offset by reduced treatment charges and higher byproduct credits from elevated copper prices.
  • Sustaining capital expenditures for 2024 were $34M, while growth capital expenditures were $17M, reflecting lower spending on Loma Larga.

Q&A

  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC: Asked about extending Ada Tepe’s mine life and employee transition plans. David Rae explained that while exploration efforts continue, any discoveries would likely require years to permit, making the transfer of resources to Čoka Rakita more practical. On employee transitions, Rae mentioned plans to train Serbian personnel at Ada Tepe.
  • Don DeMarco, National Bank Financial: Inquired about Chelopech’s improved production outlook for 2027. Rae attributed this to ongoing reviews and tighter drill spacing that optimize production plans. He also confirmed progress on the Chelopech North concession and a geological discovery at Brevene.
  • Jeremy Hoy, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about Loma Larga’s feasibility study and timeline. Rae elaborated on plans to update project economics by Q2 2025 and stated that strategic options, including partnerships or divestment, remain under consideration.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential production dip in 2027 and the impact of mine closures, indicating a slightly negative tone during Q&A. However, they acknowledged the company's financial strength and progress in growth projects.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing Čoka Rakita’s development and robust financial results. Statements like "We are very excited about Čoka Rakita's potential" (David Rae) demonstrated optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed consistent confidence, particularly in advancing Čoka Rakita and maintaining shareholder returns.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize Čoka Rakita’s timeline and exploration achievements, while the previous quarter focused on Čoka Rakita’s prefeasibility study completion.
  • Strategic focus expanded to include significant share repurchase plans for 2025, up from $50.9M in 2024.
  • Analysts’ tone remained consistent, with recurring questions about Loma Larga and exploration updates.
  • Key financial metrics, including free cash flow and cash positions, showed improvement quarter-over-quarter, reinforcing the company’s financial stability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged Ada Tepe's declining production and potential mine life challenges, emphasizing that its closure could impact short-term production.
  • Analysts inquired about VAT changes in China impacting concentrate sales. Management indicated preparedness to explore alternative markets but highlighted potential cost implications.
  • The timeline for Loma Larga remains contingent on permitting milestones, presenting a risk to its development schedule.

Final Takeaway

Dundee Precious Metals closed 2024 with robust financial results and significant progress on its key growth projects. The company targets $200M in share repurchases for 2025 and continues advancing the Čoka Rakita feasibility study, solidifying its position as a mid-tier precious metals producer. While challenges like Ada Tepe’s declining production and Loma Larga’s uncertain permitting persist, a strong cash position and disciplined capital allocation provide a solid foundation for future growth and shareholder returns.
